questions: Step 2 proposes a module-level Set for dismissed IDs. This means dismissals persist for the entire browser session (until page reload). Is that acceptable, or should dismissals reset on project switch or some other boundary?

For the 'already on timeline' check in Step 3: should we suppress the badge only when the EXACT final video (by id) is on the timeline, or also when ANY final video for that shot is on the timeline? The current plan checks by finalVideo.id which means if a newer final video is generated while an older one is on the timeline, the badge would show for the new one — which seems correct.

success_criteria.criterion: After creating a shot via createShotWithGenerationsPath or createShotWithFilesPath, the shot group appears on the timeline without requiring a manual refresh
Dismissing the final video badge persists across component remounts (scroll, resize, tab switch)
After switching to a final video via the context menu, the badge does not reappear
A new final video for a previously-dismissed shot shows the badge again
npx tsc --noEmit passes with no new errors
No changes to files outside the 4 listed key files (shotCreationPaths.ts, useFinalVideoAvailable.ts, TimelineEditor.tsx, and possibly TimelineCanvas prop types if needed)
Module-level dismissed Set does not leak memory significantly (finalVideo IDs are bounded by project size)
Manual smoke test: create shot, see badge, dismiss badge, switch to final video — all work correctly

assumptions: The useStaleVariants module-level pattern reference in the brief is about the general concept — the actual useStaleVariants uses useState, but a true module-level Set is the right approach here since we need remount survival.
The only consumers of useFinalVideoAvailable are in TimelineEditor.tsx — no other components import dismissShot or dismissedShotIds.

The generationId set on the final video asset in useSwitchToFinalVideo (finalVideo.id) is a UUID that will never collide with shot image generation_ids, so the 'already on timeline' check is reliable.

The refetchType:'inactive' on shot invalidation was likely added to avoid a double-fetch (optimistic cache + refetch), but since the refetch just confirms server state, removing it is safe and the brief overhead is acceptable.
